title: ferray-core: ArcArray only supports C-contiguous layout
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

ArcArray::from_owned() in arc.rs forces the array to standard (C) layout via as_standard_layout().into_owned() if it isn't already C-contiguous. This silently discards Fortran layout. NumPy's base-pointer mechanism preserves the original layout for views. ArcArray should either: (1) preserve the original layout and store strides properly, or (2) at minimum document that it normalizes to C order. Currently it computes strides via compute_c_strides() regardless, which would be wrong if the data were F-ordered after the force.
